texto: How paltry are the traces left behind by a life, even one concentrated around those supposed things of permanence called words. We spend our time upon the earth and then disappear, and only one one-thousandth of what we were lasts. We send all those bottles out into the ocean and so few wash up on shore.
explicacao: Citação de John Darnton sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “How paltry are the traces left behind by a life, even one concentrated around those supposed thin…” articula reflexao, 1920s, journalism em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e journalism.
